text.title: OST Build, Prune, and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Cadence for building and maintaining an OST over time, agentic subagent patterns, and common failure modes when agents edit the tree.

rule.statement: Run a tree review every two weeks: archive validated/dead branches with a date, flag opportunities with no supporting evidence after 30 days.
rule.rationale: Trees grow into unreadable wallpaper without a pruning cadence; stale branches consume planning attention without delivering signal.

rule.statement: Connect each experiment to a delivery ticket and an instrumentation plan; without both, the experiment cannot be measured.
rule.rationale: An experiment without instrumentation produces results that cannot be attributed to the intervention.

rule.statement: Agents maintain tree hygiene (classify nodes, surface stale branches, cluster duplicate opportunities, generate experiment briefs); discovery decisions stay human.
rule.rationale: Agent-proposed opportunities without evidence IDs are fiction; the agent's role is curation, not ideation.

example.note: Prompt for opportunity extraction from interviews:
example.code: Given the OST YAML below and 12 new interview snippets, propose new opportunity nodes (<=1 sentence each, customer language, no solutions). For each, list the snippet IDs supporting it. Reject any duplicates of existing opportunities (cosine similarity >=0.85).

example.note: Prompt for solution and experiment generation:
example.code: Given opportunity O.users-cant-find-export, generate three candidate solutions ranging from low to high investment. For each, propose one experiment: assumption, method, success criterion, sample size, duration.

antipattern.description: Agent proposes opportunities without evidence IDs ("users want speed").
antipattern.reason: LLMs generate plausible-sounding needs from prior knowledge; require evidence IDs from a known source store before accepting any opportunity node.

antipattern.description: Agent synthesizes experiment outcomes from prior knowledge before evidence is logged.
antipattern.reason: Require explicit "results: TBD" on all experiments until real data is recorded.

antipattern.description: Agent bulk-imports interview snippets without deduplication, flooding the tree with near-identical opportunities.
antipattern.reason: Force a similarity check (cosine >= 0.85 threshold) before adding any evidence or opportunity node.

antipattern.description: Agent overwrites hand-edits in Miro when syncing from YAML.
antipattern.reason: Require optimistic concurrency (etag/version field) on every Miro API write call.
